channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 42 killed as gunmen open fire on vehicles carrying Shiites in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Gunmen opened fire on vehicles carrying Shiite Muslims in Pakistan's restive northwest on Thursday, killing at least 42 people, including six women, and wounding 20 others in one of the region's deadliest such attacks in recent years, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disneyland Paris turns into real-life winter wonderland after season's first snowfall
item.description: The season’s first snowfall has turned Disneyland Paris into a real-life winter wonderland. Disney Parks shared a video on Thursday showing snow covering Main Street, U.S.A, Sleeping Beauty Castle, and all the festive holiday décor. It’s currently pouring snow at Disneyland Paris. pic.twitter.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: The nation is set to mark 61 years since President John F. Kennedy was assassinated as his motorcade passed through downtown Dallas on Nov. 22, 1963.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada's Trudeau to cut sales tax and send checks to millions of Canadians as election looms
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau 's government announced plans Thursday to temporarily lift the federal sales tax off a number of items and send checks to millions of Canadians who are dealing with rising costs and as a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Weird laws in Massachusetts including a $20 fine, possible jail time for frightening a pigeon
item.description: In Massachusetts, be cautious before dyeing a chick, defacing a milk carton or scaring a pigeon, as these acts are all illegal in the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp has been elected to lead the Republican Governors Association, a one-year position.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: A sheriff says a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children is in Eastern Europe but has not committed to returning home.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of teen accused in Georgia school shooting pleads not guilty to charges against him
item.description: WINDER, Ga. (AP) — The father of a 14-year-old boy accused of carrying out a deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia pleaded not guilty on Thursday to the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Critics accuse Georgia sheriff of silencing them on social media in lawsuit
item.description: Social media critics of Cobb County Sheriff Craig Owens are accusing him in a lawsuit of violating their free speech rights on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Major storm drops record rain, downs trees in Northern California after devastation further north
item.description: A major storm moving through Northern California toppled trees and dropped heavy snow and record amounts of rain after knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of people in Washington and Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Noah Berger And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | LIST
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, NewsNation

item.title: Army veteran hopes to help others with a new career on the golf course
item.description: An Army vet caddying at well-known golf resorts realized the healing effects golf could have for veterans transitioning to civilian life.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Hubbard, NewsNation

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger can face death penalty if convicted in Idaho college slayings
item.description: Bryan Kohberger, the man accused of killing four University of Idaho students in 2022, can face the death penalty, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Georgia poll worker indicted for mailing bomb threat to polling place: FBI
item.description: The FBI indicted former poll worker Nicholas Wimbish, 25, for allegedly mailing a bomb threat to a polling place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats ask FBI for files on Gaetz sex trafficking probe
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee have sent a letter to FBI Director Christopher Wray asking for “the complete evidentiary file” from the bureau's investigation into allegations of sex trafficking of minors against former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he shuttled over 500 Indian migrants into the US
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A man convicted of human smuggling testified Tuesday that he shuttled more than 500 Indian migrants across the U.S.-Canada border over four years as part of an international smuggling ring that prosecutors said led to the deaths of a family of four.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man fatally stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The older sister of Daniel Penny, the former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway, told jurors Monday that her brother was a “calm, soft-spirited person” with a reputation for honesty and integrity.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plastic bag bans can sometimes backfire: Study
item.description: Policies aimed at curbing the use of single-use plastic bags have sometimes accomplished the opposite of their intentions, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, Fox 5

item.title: Man attacked by owls twice in less than a week
item.description: Owls aren't notoriously menacing creatures, but those living in the Old Shell Road area might not agree.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, Fox 5

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9
